name: Dr Alicia Rutledge
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Planetary geochemistry • Ice-water-rock interactions • Mars analog environments
bio: Alicia M. Rutledge is an Assistant Professor of Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University whose research focuses on planetary surface processes, geochemistry, and remote sensing. Her work investigates how interactions between ice, water, and rock shape planetary surfaces, with a particular emphasis on understanding climate-driven alteration processes on Mars and Earth. Dr. Rutledge integrates spacecraft data, laboratory measurements, and field studies in extreme environments—such as glaciers and volcanic terrains—to study mineralogy and surface evolution. Her research often uses Earth analog sites, including Yellowstone and Arctic or alpine glaciers, to better interpret Martian geology. She has received recognition from NASA, including a Planetary Science Early Career Award, and contributes to mission-related research using thermal infrared and spectroscopic data.
